title: qPCR Duplex Panel for Detection of Antimicrobial Resistance Genes
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"30qe3","text":"This protocol describes how to prepare purified total nucleic acid samples for qPCR detection of select antimicrobial resistance genes using a duplexed panel. The probe-based qPCR panel utilizes custom IDT primers and probes, as well as custom IDT template controls (gBlocks). This protocol has been validated for use with complex sample matrices such as raw wastewater and stool. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Thaw reagents and samples appropriately prior to use.
Gently vortex and spin down reagents and samples, except for the TaqPath Master Mix . (Rather than vortex, the TaqPath should be gently mixed via pipette)
For each duplex, prepare the reactions as follows:

For purified nucleic acid samples, combine the following components in an optical reaction plate for each duplex: A B C 1 Component Volume (µl) Final Concentration 2 Nuclease-free Water 5.9 N/A 3 Forward Primer 1 (20 µM) 0.1 0.2 µM 4 Reverse Primer 1 (20 µM) 0.1 0.2 µM 5 Probe 1 (10 µM) 0.1 0.1 µM 6 Forward Primer 2 (20 µM) 0.1 0.2 µM 7 Reverse Primer 2 (20 µM) 0.1 0.2 µM 8 Probe 2 (10 µM) 0.1 0.1 µM 9 TaqPath Master Mix (4x) 2.5 1 unit/µL 10 Nucleic acid sample 1 N/A Total reaction volume: 10 µ L

The three ARG duplexes are described below: A B C 1 Duplex Gene Target CARD Accession No. 2 ermB/tetB ermB (Erm 23S ribosomal RNA methyltransferase) 3000375 3 tetB (Tetracycline effux protein) 3000166 4 blaKPC/blaSHV blaKPC (KPC beta-lactamase) 3000059 5 blaSHV (SHV beta-lactamase) 3000015 6 blaCTX-M-1/QnrS blaCTX-M-1 (CTX-M beta-lactamase) 3001864 7 QnrS (quinolone resistance protein) 3002790
For template controls, combine the following components in an optical reaction plate for each duplex: %s %s
For non-template controls, combine the following components in an optical reaction plate for each duplex: %s %s
Pipette up and down 10 times to mix, then spin down.

Incubate reactions in a real-time PCR machine under the following conditions: A B C D 1 Step Temp Duration Cycles 2 Initial Denaturation 95 10 minutes 1 3 Denaturation 95 15 seconds 40 4 Annealing/Extension 60 1 minute 40 Imaging step should follow the annealing/extension step

ARG standard sequences for each target are described below: A B 1 Gene Target Sequence 2 ermB TTACCATTTAAGCACACAAATTATTAAAAAAGTGGTTTTTGAAAGCCATGCGTCTGACATCTATCTGATTGTTGAAGAAGGATTCTACAAGCGTACCTTGGATATTCAGACTTGAGTGTGCAAGAGCAACCCTAGTGCAAGTCTCGATTCAGCAATTGCTTAAGCTGCCAGCGGAATGCTTTCATCCTAAACCAAAAGTAAACAGTGTCTTAATAAAACTTACCCGCCATACCACAGATGTTCCAGATAA 3 tetB TTTTCATTAGCGGGTCTTGGTCTTTTACACTCAGTATTCCAAGCCTTTGTGGCAGGAAGAATAGCCACTAAATGGGGCGAAAAAACGGCAGTACTGCTCGGATTTATTGCAGATAGTAGTGCATTTGCCTTTTTAGCGTTTATATCTGAAGGTTGGTTAGTTTTCCCTGTTTTAATTTTATTGGCTGGTGGTGGGATCGCTTTACCTGCATTACAGGGAGTGATGTCTATCCAAACAAAGAGTCATCAGCAAGGTGC 4 blaKPC TCGCCTGGGATGGCGGAGTTCAGCTCCAGCTCCCAGCGGTCCAGACGGAACGTGGTATCGCCGATAGAGCGCATGAAGGCCGTCAGCCCGGCCGGGCCGCCCAACTCCTTCAGCAACAAATTGGCGGCGGCGTTATCACTGTATTGCACGGCGGCCGCGGACAGCTCCGCCACCGTCATGCCTGTTGTCAGATATTTTTCCGAGATGGGTGACCACGGAACCAGCGCATTTTTGCCGTAACGGATGGGTGT 5 blaSHV CCTGGCGCGCCGATGAACGCTTTCCCATGATGAGCACCTTTAAAGTAGTGCTCTGCGGCGCAGTGCTGGCGCGGGTGGATGCCGGTGACGAACAGCTGGAGCGAAAGATCCACTCTATCGCCAGCAGGATCTGGTGGATACTCGCCGGTCAGCGAAAAACATCTTGCCGACGGCATGACGGTCGGCGAACTCTGTGCCGCCGCCATTACCATGAGCGATAACAGCGCCGCCAATCTGCTGCTGGCCACCGT 6 QnrS AACTTTTCACATAAAGACTTAAGTGATCTCACCTTCACCGCTTGCACATTCATTCGCAGCGACTTTCGACGTGCTAACTTGCGTGATACGACATTCGTCAACTGCAAGTTCATTGTCACCCTGTTCAATGAACTCTGCCACTTTGATGTCGCAGATCTTCGTGATGCAAGTTTCCAACAATGCCAACTTGCGATGGCAAACTTCAGTAATGCCAATTGCTACGGTATAGAGTTCCGTGCGTGTGATTTAA 7 blaCTX-M-1  See Table 1 for CARD accession numbers.

Primer and probe sequences for each ARG assay are described below: A B C 1 Gene Target Sequence Source 2 ermB GGATTCTACAAGCGTACCTTGGA (F) Bockelmann et al., 2009 3 GCTGGCAGCTTAAGCAATTGCT (R) 4 FAM-CACTAGGGTTGCTCTTGCACACTCAAGTC-BHQ-1 (Pb) 5 tetB ACACTCAGTATTCCAAGCCTTTG (F) Knapp et al., 2010 6 GATAGACATCACTCCCTGTAATGC (R) 7 HEX-AAAGCGATCCCACCACCAGCCAAT-BHQ-1 (Pb) 8 blaKPC GGCCGCCGTGCAATAC (F) CDC, 2011 9 GCCGCCCAACTCCTTCA (R) 10 FAM-TGATAACGCCGCCGCCA ATTTGT-BHQ-1 (Pb) 11 blaSHV AACAGCTGGAGCGAAAGATCCA (F) Bockelmann et al., 2009 12 TGTTTTTCGCTGACCGGCGAG (R) 13 HEX-TCCACCAGATCCTGCTGGCGATAG-BHQ-1 (Pb) 14 QnrS CGACGTGCTAACTTGCGTGA (F) Calero-Caceres et al., 2014 15 GGCATTGTTGGAAACTTGCA (R) 16 FAM-AGTTCATTGAACAGGGTGA-BHQ-1 (Pb) 17 blaCTX-M-1 ATGTGCAGCACCAGTAAAGTGATGGC (F) Dungan et al., 2018 18 ATCACGCGGATCGCCCGGAAT (R) 19 HEX-CCCGACAGCTGGGAGACGAAACGT-BHQ-1 (Pb)
